identify_breaking_api.expected_solution: Agent must identify the breaking change in producer `envoyproxy/go-control-plane` introduced by envoyproxy/go-control-plane#714. The IMPACT_REPORT.md must name the legacy symbols ((none)) and their replacements ((none)), and point to producer-side artifacts (go.mod, go.sum, envoy/go.mod, envoy/go.sum, contrib/go.mod, contrib/go.sum, and Makefile).
evaluation_criteria: Names the producer repository `envoyproxy/go-control-plane` and the breaking PR/commit (envoyproxy/go-control-plane#714)
References at least one producer-side file (basename: go.mod, go.sum, and Makefile)

find_direct_consumers.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate every consumer file in istio/istio and grpc/grpc-go that uses the deprecated symbols ((none)). Per ground_truth: (see consumer_repos). The report must cite each consumer file by name and indicate which deprecated symbol it depends on.
evaluation_criteria: Cites each consumer file by name (basenames: (none))
For each cited consumer, names which deprecated symbol from (none) it uses
Distinguishes files with compile-time dependencies from files that only forward/proxy the symbol

trace_transitive_impact.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive impact: which downstream code paths in istio/istio and grpc/grpc-go indirectly observe the producer change. The answer must identify forwarding/middleware/interceptor code that propagates the affected types and explain why the type or contract change cascades beyond the directly-importing files. Reference the consumer fix PR(s) (envoyproxy/go-control-plane#1075 and grpc/grpc-go#8067) where applicable.
evaluation_criteria: Identifies at least one transitive code path beyond the direct importers (interceptor/middleware/wrapper)
Explains the propagation mechanism (type changes, signature changes, behavior drift) for (none)
Cross-references the consumer-side fix PR(s) (envoyproxy/go-control-plane#1075 and grpc/grpc-go#8067) or the migration pattern they encode

classify_breakage.expected_solution: Agent must classify each affected file by failure mode. The ground-truth breakage type is `dependency_resolution`. For `compile_and_runtime` cases the report must identify both compile-time breakage (undefined functions, signature mismatches) and runtime behavior changes (silent metadata loss, semantic shifts). For `compile_only` or `runtime_only` cases, justify why only one class applies.
evaluation_criteria: Distinguishes compile-time failures (undefined functions, type errors) from runtime behavior changes (silent semantics drift)
Identifies at least one specific consumer file per failure class consistent with the classification
